title: athyrium spinulosum var. subtriangulare
intro: Athyrium spinulosum var. subtriangulare is a variety of the Athyrium spinulosum fern, a type of flowering plant in the wood fern family (Thelypteridaceae) native to China and Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial compounds, and is believed to have a range of health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing skin irritations, and treating respiratory issues. The plant's leaves and stems are used to prepare herbal remedies, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to address various health concerns. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of Athyrium spinulosum var. subtriangulare as a medicinal herb.
